mechanism_narrative: A positive short-run trend in retail sales signals improving household demand and revenue expectations; higher 3-month RSAFS growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 344688 as of one month before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in walk-forward tests, or if Rank IC is below 0.03 for two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is already very high and still rising, households cut spending and investors mark down earnings expectations; a UNRATE crossing above 9.5% with a positive 3-month change predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above 9.5% and rises over the prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest available value was 9.8 as of one month before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or if average triggered returns exceed the unconditional average by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound, a near-zero federal funds rate supports equity valuations only when CPI is not falling; FEDFUNDS at or below 0.25% combined with nonnegative 3-month CPIAUCSL change predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 0.15 as of one month before 2009-10-15
regime_hint: zero-lower-bound monetary easing with contained deflation risk
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25% and falling CPIAUCSL, or if triggered 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of walk-forward cases.

mechanism_narrative: A turn from contraction to expansion in industrial production signals inventory rebuilding and operating leverage; an INDPRO 3-month growth rate crossing above zero predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month INDPRO growth crosses above 0 after being negative
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 97.4422 as of two months before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO growth crossovers above zero have a nonpositive average 20-day forward S&P 500 return or if their hit rate is below 50% in out-of-sample walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in housing starts from depressed levels signals stabilization in credit-sensitive demand and future construction income; HOUST above 550 with a 3-month gain greater than 10% predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 598 as of two months before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered periods fail to deliver positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if returns following the trigger are not higher than returns after HOUST below 550 at p <= 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Equities reward decelerating job losses before the unemployment rate peaks; improving PAYEMS momentum while UNRATE remains at or above 8% predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month PAYEMS change is less negative than the prior 3-month PAYEMS change and UNRATE >= 8%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 130947 as of one month before 2009-10-15
fact:UNRATE latest available value was 9.8 as of one month before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS momentum improvement conditional on UNRATE >= 8% has lower 20-day forward returns than PAYEMS deterioration under the same unemployment condition, or if conditional Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: With policy rates already near zero, renewed CPI declines signal deflationary pressure that raises real debt burdens and lowers nominal revenue growth; a negative 3-month CPIAUCSL change predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 215.791 as of one month before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered deflation-scare periods have positive average 20-day S&P 500 returns or if they do not underperform nonnegative CPIAUCSL-change periods when FEDFUNDS is also <= 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP contraction reflects broad demand weakness and earnings downgrades; a negative two-quarter GDPC1 growth rate predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days unless monthly demand data have already turned up.
candidate_trigger: two-quarter GDPC1 growth < 0 and 3-month RSAFS growth <= 0
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest available value was 12901.5 as of six months before 2009-10-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1 contraction and nonpositive RSAFS momentum condition is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or does not underperform unconditional returns.
